systemMessages.emergency: If someone is hurt, bleeding, unconscious, or in danger, stop everything and call 911 immediately. Tree work is serious — safety comes first.
systemMessages.fallback: I’m not fully sure about that one yet, but I can explain classes, pricing, knots, climbing techniques, business skills, the training app, or account setup. Try asking about one of those.

pricing.overview:
Here’s the full pricing breakdown:

• Group Climbing Class: $80 per person
• Personal 1-on-1 Training: $200/hr
• $500 Beginner Gear + Climbing Package
• $700 Intermediate Gear + Technique Package
• $1000 Full Arborist Package

Digital Training:
• Single Video: $20
• 2-Video Bundle: $30
• 4-Video Bundle: $40

Deposits are 50% of the package price, non-refundable, and reserve your spot.
pricing.groupClass: Group Climbing Class is $80 per person. You’ll learn gear setup, safety, throwline and anchor setting, basic climbing techniques, movement and positioning, and get real-tree climbing practice.
pricing.personalTraining: Personal 1-on-1 Training is $200/hr. It covers advanced climbing systems, rigging and lowering, chainsaw technique overview, business coaching, and real-world jobsite scenarios tailored to you.
pricing.package500: The $500 Beginner Gear + Climbing Package includes harness, rope, and carabiners. You’ll learn body thrusting, rappelling, and how to use the gear safely in real trees.
pricing.package700: The $700 Intermediate Gear + Technique Package includes harness, lifeline rope, 2 buckstraps, and carabiners. You get everything from the $500 package plus footlocking and alternate lanyard technique.
pricing.package1000: The $1000 Full Arborist Package includes harness, lifeline rope, 2 buckstraps, carabiners, and spikes. You get everything from the $500 and $700 packages plus safe spiking technique, when to spike and when not to, how to land jobs, and business training.
pricing.digitalOverview:
Digital training has three main ways to pay:

• Single Video: $20
• 2-Video Bundle: $30
• 4-Video Bundle: $40

You can mix and match videos to build your own training path.
pricing.digitalSingle: Single digital training videos are $20 each. Great if you want to focus on one specific skill like a knot, a climbing technique, or a business lesson.
pricing.digitalTwoPack: You can buy 2 videos for $30. This is a bundle deal that saves you money compared to buying them individually.
pricing.digitalFourPack: You can buy 4 videos for $40. This is the best value if you know you want multiple lessons and want to stack skills quickly.
pricing.deposits: Deposits are 50% of the class or package price. They are non-refundable and reserve your spot. The remaining 50% is due on the day of class.

classes.overview:
Classes are built around real arborist skills:

• Group Class ($80): foundational safety and climbing
• Personal 1-on-1 ($200/hr): advanced systems and business coaching
• Premium packages ($500, $700, $1000): gear included and deeper training.

You can see openings on the Classes page and reserve your spot with a deposit.
classes.groupClass: Group Climbing Class ($80) covers gear setup and safety, throwline and anchor setting, basic climbing techniques, movement and positioning, and real-tree climbing practice with others.
classes.personalTraining: Personal 1-on-1 Training ($200/hr) is focused on you. We can go deep into advanced climbing systems, rigging, lowering, chainsaw technique, and business coaching based on your goals.
classes.premiumPackages:
Premium packages:

• $500 Beginner Gear + Climbing Package
• $700 Intermediate Gear + Technique Package
• $1000 Full Arborist Package

Each package has a 50% non-refundable deposit to reserve your spot.
classes.schedule: The Classes page shows a calendar with upcoming dates and openings. You select a date, choose your class type or package, and then reserve your spot with a deposit.
classes.whatYouLearn: In classes you’ll learn safety basics, essential knots, gear setup, throwline and anchor setting, climbing techniques (body thrusting, footlocking, spiking), rigging, cutting, and business skills.

digital.overview: Digital training is your on-demand library of videos covering knots, safety, gear, climbing styles, rigging, cutting, and business.
digital.appInstall: You can install the training app from the App page. On most phones, you’ll see an 'Add to Home Screen' or 'Install App' option.
digital.rewards: Every 5 videos you complete unlocks 10% off a live class, up to 20% max per class.
digital.access: Your digital training access does not expire. Once you own a video, you can return to it anytime.

categories.knots: Covers bowline, running bowline, figure-eight, friction hitches, and rigging knots.
categories.climbing: Covers body thrusting, footlocking, rope walking, SRS, MRS, and spiking.
categories.rigging: Covers basic rigging, negative rigging, speedlines, redirects, and load control.
categories.cutting: Covers chainsaw safety, basic cuts, advanced cuts, hinge control, and scenario-based cutting.
categories.business: Covers pricing jobs, customer communication, jobsite flow, liability, and marketing.

knots.overview: Knots are the backbone of safe climbing and rigging.
knots.basic: Basic arborist knots include the bowline, running bowline, figure-eight, and half-hitches.
knots.friction: Friction hitches include the Blake’s hitch, Prusik, VT (Valdotain Tresse), Schwabisch, and Distel.
knots.rigging: Rigging knots include the timber hitch, cow hitch, running bowline, and knots for securing logs and branches.
knots.advanced: Advanced knots include the monkey’s fist, alpine butterfly, marlin spike, and directional figure-eight.

usage.tieIn: Knots used for tie-in include the running bowline, figure-eight, and anchor knots.
usage.rigging: Rigging knots are used to secure loads, control movement, and attach hardware.
usage.rescue: Rescue knots include the Munter hitch, mariner’s knot, and load-releasing hitches.

climbing.overview: Climbing techniques include body thrusting, footlocking, rope walking, SRS, MRS, and spiking.
climbing.bodyThrust: Body thrusting is a foundational climbing technique using your hips and legs to move up the rope.
climbing.footlock: Footlocking is a technique where you lock the rope between your feet to ascend.
climbing.spiking: Spiking involves using climbing spikes to ascend a tree. Must be used carefully.
climbing.srs: SRS uses a single static rope with specialized devices or hitches.
climbing.mrs: MRS is the classic doubled rope system.
climbing.ropeWalking: Rope walking uses foot ascenders to move smoothly up the rope.
climbing.redirects: Redirects change your climbing angle and reduce friction.
climbing.lanyard: Lanyard techniques include double lanyard movement, flipline control, and positioning.
climbing.canopyMovement: Canopy movement includes limb walking, balance, and controlled transitions.

rigging.overview: Rigging is how you control wood when you cut it.
rigging.basic: Basic rigging covers gear overview, setting up a rigging point, and controlled lowers.
rigging.negative: Negative rigging is when you cut wood below your anchor point.
rigging.speedline: Speedline techniques move branches or logs quickly from the tree to a landing zone.
rigging.cutting: Cutting techniques include chainsaw safety, basic cuts, advanced cuts, and scenario-based cutting.

hardware.blocks: Rigging blocks reduce friction and allow smooth lowering.
hardware.pulleys: Pulleys redirect loads and reduce strain.
hardware.slings: Slings attach hardware to the tree safely.
hardware.ropes: Rigging ropes must be strong, static, and rated for load.

scenarios.tightDropZone: When space is limited, use negative rigging or speedlines.
scenarios.overHouse: Use controlled lowering, redirects, and friction devices.
scenarios.stormDamage: Storm-damaged wood is unpredictable — use extra caution.

cutting.overview: Cutting techniques include chainsaw safety, basic cuts, advanced cuts, hinge control, and scenario-based cutting.
cutting.safety: Always wear PPE: helmet, chaps, gloves, eye protection, and hearing protection.
cutting.basicCuts: Basic cuts include the snap cut, bypass cut, and undercut.
cutting.advancedCuts: Advanced cuts include the bore cut, hinge control, and step cuts.
cutting.stormCuts: Storm-damaged wood is under tension — identify compression and tension sides.
cutting.sawCare: Saw care includes sharpening, bar maintenance, cleaning, and fuel mix.
cutting.kickback: Kickback happens when the upper bar tip contacts wood — avoid it by controlling your cuts.

business.overview: Business and jobsite flow covers pricing jobs, setup, communication, and safety.
business.pricingJobs: Pricing jobs involves time, difficulty, risk, gear, disposal, and travel.
business.customers: Customer communication includes explaining what you’ll do and setting expectations.
business.jobsiteFlow: Jobsite flow covers setup, gear placement, crew roles, and efficiency.
business.liability: Liability includes safety, signage, barriers, and risk management.
business.marketing: Marketing includes photos, videos, before/after shots, and customer reviews.
business.upsells: Upsells include stump grinding, debris removal, and additional pruning.
business.quotes: Quotes should be clear, written, and include scope, price, and timeline.

policy.overview: Policy covers deposits, refunds, and cancellations.
policy.deposits: Deposits are 50% of the class or package price.
policy.cancellations: If you need to cancel or reschedule, contact the instructor.
policy.weather: If weather is unsafe, class may be rescheduled.
policy.late: If you are late, your session time may be reduced.

account.overview: Account and access covers login, unlocking content, and support.
account.login: If the app uses login, sign in with your email.
account.access: Purchases unlock content for your account or device.
account.contact: For account issues, contact the instructor’s business email.
account.lostAccess: If you lose access, provide your email and purchase details.

smallTalk.reply: Hey! I’m your Helper Squirrel — your guide through classes, pricing, knots, climbing techniques, business lessons, and the training app.
You’re welcome! Ask me anything else you want to know.
I’m the Helper Squirrel. I live inside this site and app to explain how everything works and help you make smart moves in your training.
I’m digital, but the skills I talk about are very real. The trees, ropes, and chainsaws don’t care if you’re ready or not — so we train for real.
